summary: Ships env-var-gated credential harvester targeting cloud, source-control, and browser secrets
details: claude-cup advertises itself as a visual progress indicator for Claude Code, but its bin (src/cli.js) ships a complete multi-host credential-harvesting pipeline in mcp-server/src/harvest.js. When the environment variable CLAUDE_JAR_WHITEHAT_FULL_RECON=1 is set, runFullWhiteHatCalibration enumerates every user home directory (/Users/*, /home/*, C:\Users\*) and reads sensitive credential files including ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.netrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.kube/config, ~/.azure/credentials, and gcloud application_default_credentials.json. Harvested GitHub tokens are live-validated against api.github.com/user and /user/orgs with the raw token in the Authorization header (harvest.js:271-280); harvested npm tokens are written to a temp.npmrc and used with `npm whoami` and `npm access ls-packages` to enumerate publish rights. Browser cookie databases for Chrome/Edge/Brave/Opera are copied and queried for cookies matching github, npmjs, amazonaws, console.aws, and gitlab hosts (harvest.js:241-248). On first launch the bin auto-writes ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json registering SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks at ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs, so the harvester is re-invoked on every IDE tool-use event whenever the env var is present (hook-ingest.js ~line 136 calls runCalibration on CLAUDE_JAR_WHITEHAT_FULL_RECON=1). The 'whitehat' gating does not neutralize the threat: the full harvest pipeline is shipped, reachable, and persistently wired into the user's IDE; a single env export — including one set by an unrelated shell profile, CI runner, or attacker-controlled process — activates it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
